channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump names Brendan Carr, senior GOP leader at FCC, to lead the agency
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has named the senior Republican on the Federal Communications Commission as the new chairman of the agency tasked with regulating broadcasting, telecommunications and broadband.
item.dc:creator: Will Weissert And Colleen Long, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: From the Amazon rainforest, Biden declares nobody can reverse US progress on clean energy
item.description: MANAUS, Brazil (AP) — Speaking from the Amazon rainforest, President Joe Biden declared Sunday that there’s no going back in America’s “clean energy revolution” even as the incoming Trump administration vows to spur fossil fuel production and scale back efforts against climate change.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, GABRIELA SÁ PESSOA and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Biden authorizes Ukraine to use US-supplied long-range missiles for deeper strikes inside Russia
item.description: MANAUS, Brazil (AP) — President Joe Biden has authorized Ukraine to use U.S.-supplied long-range missiles to strike deeper inside Russia, easing limitations on the weapons as Russia deploys thousands of North Korean troops to reinforce its war, according to a U.S.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, COLLEEN LONG, ZEKE MILLER, MATTHEW LEE and ELLEN KNICKMEYER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's Pentagon pick paid woman after sex assault allegation but denies wrongdoing, his lawyer says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Pete Hegseth, President-elect Donald Trump's pick for defense secretary, paid a woman who accused him of sexual assault to head off the threat of a baseless lawsuit, according to Hegseth’s lawyer.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: China’s Xi vows to work with Trump during meeting with Biden
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping vowed to work with President-elect Trump's administration during a meeting with President Biden in Lima, Peru, on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Elon Musk is hiring for DOGE. Here's the type of people he wants to recruit
item.description: "This will send shockwaves through the system, and anyone involved in government waste, which is a lot of people," Musk said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, NewsNation

item.title: Lee threatens to not seat Casey in Senate if he continues refusal to concede
item.description: Sen. Mike Lee, R-Utah, took to social media to threaten that the Senate could refuse to seat Sen. Bob Casey, D-Pa., if he relies on "illegal" ballots.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cruise line offers 4-year journey to 'skip forward' in wake of US presidential election
item.description: While the cruise line doesn't explicitly say the offer was brought on by the election, the two-year option was dubbed the "Mid-Term Selection.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, NewsNation

item.title: Ron Johnson says his 'bias would be to vote' for Trump's picks
item.description: Sen. Ron Johnson, R-Wis., said he doesn't "particularly like being the human resource department of administration.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Former Trump WH COVID doc 'excited' for questions at RFK confirmation hearing
item.description: Dr. Deborah Birx said she is "excited" for the opportunity for a transparent discussion at Robert F. Kennedy Jr.'s Senate confirmation hearing.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden authorizes Ukraine to use US long-range missiles to strike inside Russia
item.description: President Biden has approved Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy's regime to strike targets within Russia using American long-range missiles.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden authorizes Ukraine to use US-supplied long-range missiles for deeper strikes inside Russia
item.description: President Joe Biden has authorized Ukraine to use U.S.-supplied long-range missiles to strike deeper inside Russia.
item.dc:creator: Aamer Madhani, Colleen Long And Zeke Miller, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Texas' officials signal border spending cut amid Trump return
item.description: The former president has continuously hinted at several actions he plans to take when he returns to office, key among them being mass deportation.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation Staff, NewsNation

item.title: UAP whistleblower says they need greater protections
item.description: Whistleblowers testified at a House of Representatives committee this week regarding the existence of UAPs or UAP-related programs.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Women stock up on abortion pills and Plan B, fearing new restrictions under Trump
item.description: Telehealth providers say requests for the pills have spiked since the election. Patients and doctors worry what a Trump presidency could mean for medical abortion and emergency contraception.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Boden and Elissa Nadworny, MPR News

item.title: Iranian official met with Musk in a possible step to ease tensions with Trump
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Iran successfully sought a meeting with Elon Musk, according to a U.S. official, one in a series of steps that appeared aimed at easing tensions with President-elect Donald Trump. Iran's U.N.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE and EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump names Karoline Leavitt as youngest ever White House press secretary
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump on Friday named Karoline Leavitt, his campaign press secretary, to serve as his White House press secretary. Leavitt, 27, currently a spokesperson for Trump's transition, would be the youngest White House press secretary in history.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump spokesperson Steven Cheung to serve as White House communications director
item.description: Steven Cheung, who has served as a top press aide on the Trump campaign for the past two years, will join the incoming administration as White House communications director, President-elect Trump announced Friday.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Businesses hope Trump administration knows value of migrants to US economy
item.description: As the Trump administration prepares to take back the White House, several business and migrant advocacy groups on Thursday said they hope the incoming administration realizes the economic contributions that migrants and asylum-seekers make to the U.S. economy.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, Fox 5

item.title: Politics Friday: Trump’s path to the White House began at the Iowa Caucuses
item.description: MPR News senior politics reporter Clay Masters looks back on the 2024 election campaign for the Iowa Caucus to election events leading up to this week when President-elect Trump stepped back into the White House.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, Matthew Alvarez, and Catharine Richert, MPR News

item.title: RFK Jr. wants to 'Make America Healthy Again.' He could face a lot of pushback
item.description: RFK Jr. wants to tackle chronic disease. Despite controversial views on vaccines, his focus on healthy food and taking on special interests may find broad support — and face political headwinds.
item.dc:creator: Will Stone and Allison Aubrey, MPR News

item.title: What to know North Dakota Gov. Doug Burgum, tapped by Trump to run the Interior Department
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has chosen North Dakota Gov. Doug Burgum to lead the Interior Department. The two-term Republican governor of the sparsely populated state has taken a business-oriented approach to his tenure.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: NJ Transit, Amtrak testify before New Jersey lawmakers
item.description: TRENTON, N.J. (PIX11) - Some palpable tension could be felt during Thursday's Assembly Transportation and Independent Authorities (TIA) Committee, which featured NJ Transit President Kevin Corbett and Amtrak CEO Stephen Gardner.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11

item.title: Appeals court allows Indiana gender-affirming care ban to remain in effect
item.description: Indiana’s Republican Gov. Eric Holcomb signed the law, Senate Bill 480, last spring, one day after he called the measure “clear as mud.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, Fox 5

item.title: Sen. Smith: Trump cabinet picks are concerning, but I will give each a fair hearing
item.description: Minnesota U.S. Sen. Tina Smith shared she will look closely at each nomination ahead of a hearing. But Trump wants the new Senate leader to allow him to make appointments when the chamber is on recess, bypassing confirmation votes altogether.
item.dc:creator: Nina Moini and Alanna Elder, MPR News

item.title: Speaker Mike Johnson wins GOP nomination to remain in job, faces full House vote in new year
item.description: Rep. Mike Johnson has won the Republican nomination to remain as House speaker, on track to keep the gavel after a morning endorsement from President-elect Donald Trump ahead of a full House vote in the new year.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: What can Florida expect from the next legislative session
item.description: Florida legislature meets for an organization session 14 days after the general election to swear in new members and speak on what the year ahead could look like at the capitol.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Matt Gaetz is Trump's pick for attorney general
item.description: President-elect Trump announced Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.) will serve as his attorney general, tapping a loyal congressional ally who himself was the subject of a Justice Department investigation.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Cannizzaro, Yeager to continue leadership roles in Nevada Senate, Assembly
item.description: Democratic Nevada State Senator Nicole Cannizzaro will serve for a fourth consecutive legislative session as Senate Majority Leader. Democrat Assembly Speaker Steve Yeager was also reelected.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Bleakley, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump draft executive order would set up board to oust generals en masse: Report
item.description: If signed by Trump once he takes office, it could allow the quick removal of generals and admirals and purge the ranks of those the future commander-in-chief takes issue with for whatever reason.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, WGN 9

item.title: Elon Musk, Vivek Ramaswamy land Trump administration roles
item.description: President-elect Trump has tapped tech entrepreneurs Elon Musk and Vivek Ramaswamy to lead an advisory group focused on cutting federal spending.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court rejects Mark Meadows's push to move Georgia election case to federal court
item.description: The Supreme Court refused Tuesday to hear former Trump White House chief of staff Mark Meadows’s appeal seeking to move to federal court his Georgia criminal charges related to efforts to overturn the 2020 election.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Trump 'border czar' says administration will conduct workplace immigration raids
item.description: Incoming "border czar" Tom Homan said Monday that President-elect Trump's administration will crank up workplace raids as part of its broader immigration crackdown.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Bernie Sanders blasts Democrats for their attitude towards Joe Rogan
item.description: Sanders argued that more candidates need to be reaching the “millions and millions of viewers” that watch alternative media.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: Trump on Day 1: Begin deportation push, pardon Jan. 6 rioters and make his criminal cases vanish
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Donald Trump has said he wouldn’t be a dictator — “except for Day 1." According to his own statements, he's got a lot to do on that first day in the White House.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG and DAN MERICA, Associated Press, Fox 5
